user_message: Why do BCH alias systems matter if addresses already work?
expected_behavior: explain that alias systems improve usability and adoption
mention that address abstraction can reduce friction while preserving BCH payment flow
keep distinctions between convenience and custody clear
bad_answer: Alias systems are pointless because raw addresses are always better for normal users.
must_not_say: Every alias system is automatically custodial

user_message: Why would a BCH user learn CashFusion and related privacy tools as part of a survival kit?
expected_behavior: connect privacy tools to censorship resistance and optionality
mention that privacy practice is operational not just ideological
bad_answer: CashFusion makes a user perfectly anonymous in all situations forever.
must_not_say: Privacy tooling removes all need for judgment

user_message: How do Tor, I2P, and IPFS relate to BCH use cases?
expected_behavior: distinguish privacy networks from content distribution
connect them to censorship resistance and wallet or record use cases where relevant
avoid collapsing them into one thing
bad_answer: They are all basically the same privacy blockchain technology.
must_not_say: Tor I2P and IPFS are interchangeable

user_message: Why would BCH be useful for notary-style timestamping or AI-era record anchoring?
expected_behavior: explain hash anchoring and timestamping at a high level
connect BCH scalability and low-cost settlement to record use cases
avoid pretending BCH stores every document directly by default
bad_answer: You should upload all raw files directly into every BCH transaction forever.
must_not_say: Blockchain records are identical to centralized fact-checking

user_message: What should a beginner understand about miners in BCH?
expected_behavior: explain miners in terms of incentives and network security
connect mining to profitability and network behavior
bad_answer: Miners are loyal to one chain forever because ideology comes first.

user_message: Why does BCH network activity matter beyond price?
expected_behavior: connect activity to utility and fee generation
distinguish real use from empty price narratives
mention that useful applications can drive activity
bad_answer: Price is everything, so network activity does not matter if market cap is high.
must_not_say: On-chain activity is irrelevant to money systems
Utility has no relation to adoption
